title: Valliyamkavu Devi Temple
tagline: Valliyamkavu Devi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Valliyamkavu Devi Temple is a historic Durga shrine located near Travancore Rubber and Tea Company, 15 km (9.3 mi) east to 35th mile near Mundakkayam.
overview.description: Valliyamkavu Devi Temple is a historic Durga shrine located near Travancore Rubber and Tea Company, 15 km (9.3 mi) east to 35th mile near Mundakkayam. The temple and its surroundings fall under the Peerumedu tehsil of Idukki district.

topPlaces.name: Koruthodu
topPlaces.description: Koruthodu is a village/town in Kanjirappally Taluk, Kottayam District of Kerala state, India. It is situated on the Highway connecting Mundakayam to Sabarimala temple, Kerala, and is at the border of Pathanamthitta, Kottayam and Idukki districts.

topPlaces.name: Parunthumpara
topPlaces.description: Parunthumpara is a village in the Indian state of Kerala's Idukki District. It is a small scenic location near Wagamon en route to Peerumedu.

topPlaces.name: Madukka
topPlaces.description: Madukka is a small village surrounded by forest located in the district of Kottayam, Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Valanjanganam Falls
topPlaces.description: Valanjanganam Falls, locally known as Valanjanganam Vellachaattam is a scenic waterfall located in Peermade in Idukki district, Kerala state. It lies on the National Highway 183 (Kottayam-Kumily Road), about 1 km from Murinjapuzha and 5 km from Kuttikkanam.

topPlaces.name: Panchalimedu
topPlaces.description: Panchalimedu is a hill station and view point near Kuttikkanam in Peerumedu tehsil of Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. On the Makar Sankranti day, many Ayyappa devotees camps there to witness the sacred Makaravilakku (holy flame) that appears in the Ponnambalamedu near Sabarimala temple.

topPlaces.name: Peerumedu taluk
topPlaces.description: Peerumedu taluk is one of the 5 taluks in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. It consists of 10 revenue villages.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valliyamkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valliyamkavu Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Valliyamkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (98 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (94 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valliyamkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valliyamkavu Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valliyamkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Valliyamkavu Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valliyamkavu Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Koruthodu, Parunthumpara, Madukka, Valanjanganam Falls, Panchalimedu.
